# Adverse 'add' inputs all surface specific exit codes. Stable codes
# are the contract LLMs and shell pipelines rely on.

# No args at all → cobra's arg-count check (exit 2).
! exec eon add
stderr 'Usage error.*requires at least'

# No schedule flag (but command provided) → exit 2 (usage).
! exec eon add -- echo hi
stderr 'provide exactly one of --cron or --at'

# Both schedule flags → exit 2.
! exec eon add --cron '@hourly' --at '+1h' -- echo hi
stderr 'provide exactly one of --cron or --at'

# Missing '--' → exit 2.
! exec eon add --cron '@hourly' echo hi
stderr 'Usage error.*place the command'

# Garbage cron expression → exit 5 (precondition).
! exec eon add --cron 'garbage' -- echo hi
stderr 'invalid cron'

# Empty cron → exit 5.
! exec eon add --cron '' -- echo hi

# Past one-shot → exit 5.
! exec eon add --at '2020-01-01T00:00:00Z' -- echo hi
stderr 'not in the future'

# Nonsense at value → exit 5.
! exec eon add --at 'yesterday' -- echo hi
stderr 'invalid time'

# Negative day offset → exit 5.
! exec eon add --at '+-1h' -- echo hi
